prompt: A loud heckler near the front is throwing off your artists. How do you handle it?

answers.label: Engage them — turn it into a moment

outcomes.description: Your artist fires back with a perfect comeback. The crowd erupts.
The exchange falls flat. The heckler doubles down.
Security handles it quietly. The show continues without a hitch.
The removal causes a scene. Half the crowd sides with the heckler.
The artist stays locked in. The heckler eventually gives up.
The distraction lingers. The performance suffers.

prompt: The crowd is chanting for an encore. You're not sure if your artists have the energy.

outcomes.description: They dig deep and deliver. The room ends on a high.
Fatigue shows. The encore feels like a formality.

answers.label: End the show — leave them wanting more

outcomes.description: Classic move. The buzz lasts all week.

prompt: A guitar string snaps mid-song. There's a spare in the case but it'll take a minute.

answers.label: Change it on stage — make it part of the show

outcomes.description: The crowd cheers them on. An authentic moment captured on a dozen phones.
The pause kills the momentum. People start checking their phones.
The backup is badly out of tune. The next song sounds rough.

prompt: Halfway through, your artist wants to drop a planned song and freestyle instead.

outcomes.description: A raw, electric moment. The crowd feels like they witnessed something real.
Rough around the edges but the crowd respects the effort.
They lose the flow completely. An awkward stumble mid-set.
Solid, professional performance. Everything goes to plan.
